title: Amphi-Hydrus

The '''Amphi-Hydrus''' were an [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibian]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. They were proficient in using [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] fueled by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to manipulate the [[Brain/Legends|minds]] of others. At one point, they telepathically controlled several imported [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]], causing a brief period of chaos on Dac. Following the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugation]] of the planet, the Amphi-Hydrus retreated to living in its [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Amphi-Hydrus were<ref name="Galaxy7" /> one of several<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]<ref name="Galaxy7" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Calamari sector/Legends|Calamari sector]].<ref name="TEA" /> They were small, physically weak [[Amphibian/Legends|amphibian]] beings with a squat body plan, [[Color/Legends|green]] and brown skin, and orange [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. They had four double-jointed [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], with the hind limbs' toes being light-colored and blunt and the forelimbs featuring four sharp, black [[Claw/Legends|claws]] each. The Amphi-Hydrus were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], being able to tap into its [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]].<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==Society and culture==
The Amphi-Hydrus were highly adept in the use of [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]], using the dark side of the Force to manipulate the [[Brain/Legends|minds]] of other beings. After their brief reign of terror on their homeworld ended, the species bitterly awaited another chance to regain power.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==History==
At one point, the Amphi-Hydrus established telepathic control over several<ref name="Galaxy7" /> semi-sentient<ref name="TEA" /> [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] that had been imported on Dac. Riding on their backs, the Amphi-Hydrus ushered in a brief period of chaos on their homeworld. When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugated]] the planet<ref name="Galaxy7" /> around [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' establishes that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] occupation of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], which ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' states lasted for nearly two [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]], [[Calamarian Revolt|ended]] in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. Therefore, the Imperial [[Subjugation of Mon Calamari|subjugation]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] must have occurred not long after the [[Declaration of a New Order|establishment]] of the Empire, or around [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> however, the species retreated to the [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]] of Dac, where they waited to gain power again.<ref name="Galaxy7" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Amphi-Hydrus were created by [[Daren Horley]] through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition run in the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]''.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> The competition, launched in the magazine's [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|third issue]]<ref name="Galaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|3|Design An Alien|Design An Alien Contest}}</ref> in [[April]] [[1995]],<ref name="DateGalaxy3">{{GalaxyCite|2|Rebel Report}}</ref> called for readers to create completely new [[Alien/Legends|alien]] species inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. Harley was twenty-nine years of age when they submitted their entry on the Amphi-Hydrus, which was later published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]]<ref name="Galaxy7" /> in April [[1996]],<ref name="DateGalaxy7">{{GalaxyCite|6|Around the Galaxy}}</ref> under the "honorable mentions" section of the "16 and up" subdivision.<ref name="Galaxy7" /> [[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], later stated that published material from the "Design An Alien" competition was considered [[canon]] with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|author=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Yes, though any text discrepencies [sic] with other sources would [sic] likely to superceded [sic] by the other source.}} {{C|In response to: "Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon?"}}</ref>
